reviews.author: N

reviews.author: Jacob Feck
reviews.text: The Social is easily one of the best student spots in town! The atmosphere is always lively, and the management goes above and beyond to organize events that actually make it feel like a community. If you want a place where it's easy to meet people and still have a great space to study, this is it.

reviews.author: Sarah Guardia
reviews.text: Very clean and comfortable room, can control the AC if it gets too hot! LOVED the bathroom, very spacious with dim lighting and a makeup mirror. Soap smelled GREAT. Definitely recommend doing the valet parking ($61), all other parking options we found weren’t as good.

reviews.author: Alex Jones
reviews.text: Amazing stay and very good value. I was upgraded free due to platinum status to a deluxe suite and it was incredible for the price I paid. reviews.author: Ethan Zhang
reviews.text: Emna, the leasing agent was one of the nicest we have ever encountered on a visit touring any properties near USC. Would highly recommend the apartments there because I guarantee the staff alone will make you feel right at home.

reviews.author: KoDa Franklin
reviews.text: My roommates and I recently moved in and Campus Prime is hands down the best student housing in Syracuse! Responsive staff, convenient location, community feel, and we love the natural lighting in our apartment!

reviews.author: Kobe
reviews.text: Love this place! You can really tell a lot of care went into designing and building these homes. The attention to detail makes it a great place to live!

reviews.author: Dejuan Franklin
reviews.text: Ownership and Management here is absolutely amazing — they truly care about the community and it shows in everything they do. Our residents, especially student groups like fraternities and sororities, love living here because of the welcoming environment and supportive team. It’s a great place to call home away from ho

reviews.author: Drew B
reviews.text: Our AC broke in May, so we put in a service request right away. After a few weeks of our apartment being 90 degrees, they finally brought us a window AC unit. Except, our electricity bill was outrageous (well over $200 a month for a tiny apartment). It must have been August before they finally bothered to fix the centr

reviews.author: Hannah Schoenholtz
reviews.text: This probably isn't the worst place to live in Boulder as a student, but, I have been extremely dissatisfied with my time at U Club and am very much looking forward to finding new housing this summer when my lease is finally up. While I have so many problems with this building like the hot tub being unusable for 95% of

reviews.author: Graham
reviews.text: To parents and CU students, please take the time to read this review as someone who lived at U Club for two years. First, the pros are the amazing gym, pool/hot tub were nice (but broken most of the time), and we were lucky to have a nice mountain view. It is very close to campus, so super convenient. For the cons, ove

reviews.author: Amy Rose
reviews.text: My daughter is moving in to Storyboard next month and she is so excited! Since we first contacted Storyboard in April, everyone has been great to work with. Molly goes out of her way to answer questions and just give of her time, and Walker has been great too with answering all of our questions and giving us a tour. Ce

reviews.author: Riley
reviews.text: Molly was extremely helpful and thorough with both the tour and move-in. The team was very kind and answered any questions. Easiest move I’ve made.

reviews.author: M Sc
reviews.text: Along with the walls being thin, the constant noise above you, and the deafening train passing by at all hours, the management is atrocious. The property manager has been rude to students of color and has made it a point to ignore complaints and phone calls when they reach out. Valerie is dismissive and condescending.

reviews.author: Selina Quattlebaum
reviews.text: I love these apartments! I lived here a few years ago and only left because I moved out of state. When I came back to AZ I knew I wanted to come back! reviews.author: Ryan Poulson
reviews.text: Stopped by Island Palms today during our property outreach and had the chance to meet Stormi, the community manager. Professional, kin

reviews.text: I’ve had a great experience living at Island Palm Apartment Homes in Mesa. reviews.author: Julie Averbach

One amazing perk is that you can attend events in the building (including talks by high-profi

reviews.author: Layla G
reviews.text: I love living here. This is my last year of college, and I’m really happy to be spending it in this building. It’s well-kept, clean, and in a beautiful, quiet area. The cleaning and maintenance staff are quick and reliable, and the front desk team is always friendly and helpful. Bella and Luis are kind and responsive.

reviews.author: Chris barahona
reviews.text: I love living here. The building is always clean, well-maintained, and in a really nice, quiet area. The views are amazing and the overall atmosphere makes it a great place to come back to every day. The staff does a great job keeping everything running smoothly, and any issues are handled quickly. Bella and Luis are e

reviews.author: Josh
reviews.text: The locations for these apartments are great. That’s all that’s great about them. They SEEM to be a fair price. Not to mention the expensive electric bill no matter the time

reviews.author: Josh Stephens
reviews.text: Trotters Park is a great place to live as it is close to the Short North and Grandview but away from all the noise. The new management (Ren and Brandi) are fantastic. Not only do they care about you as a human (not just a resident), but they are always actively seeking ways to improve the property and bring the complex

reviews.author: Derek Abner
reviews.text: With new management there is always a transition period, but the new staff here at Trotter's Park have been very kind and welcoming. Excited to see the property in good hands and maintenance is quick to address maintenance issues. I would highly recommend Trotter's Park.

reviews.author: Peter R.
reviews.text: Gateway Lofts is THE best!! I've stayed at Gateway for the past year and have nothing but positive things to say about the apartment complex, especially the management team. The apartment complex itself is beautiful; everything is new and spacious within the apartment units. They also have great amenities, including a

reviews.author: Adam Kirtley
reviews.text: My name is Adam (AK), and I currently live at Gateway Lofts Cincinnati while attending University of Cincinnati. I’ve had a great experience living here. reviews.author: Keri Reilly

The property is beautiful, clean, and well maintained, and any issues are handled quickly and profess

It seems to be faulty, yet the issue keeps happening repeatedly. The mana

reviews.author: Grace Lien
reviews.text: We (a UCB student and parents) went for a housing tour. Yaselle showed us around to check the amenities and environment. It seemed safe and convenient to walk to school. We are considering to rent an unit there.

reviews.author: Courtney Moore
reviews.text: We’ve lived here since the beginning of Covid. Ardent wasn’t horrible but they weren’t great either. In the few months coastal ridge has taken over, it has been fantastic. My repeated interactions with Talea have been particularly concerning. Her tone during phone conversations is consistently irritated, dismissive, and impatient. Rather than

reviews.author: Jenny Burnham
reviews.text: WOW! Not a detail missed in this gorgeous home! Every corner we turned, we were amazed at how cute and spacious the home is! reviews.author: Julie Jeppson
reviews.text: Had a WONDERFUL experience at The Guest House, celebrating my mother-in-laws 80th birthday. We had 11 people and were more than comfortable in our own rooms. All the amenities were top-notch with the highest quality in furniture, bedding, kitchenware, etc. Location was perfect to all the restaurants, shops, and grocery

reviews.author: Mark Schabert
reviews.text: We stayed here for a one night anniversary vacation and time to ourselves, as we have seven children and live just a few minutes away, but didn't want to leave the kids for too long or be too far away from them in case they needed anything. We had been hoping to enjoy the lake and bike trails, but due to the unseasonab

reviews.author: Anthony Osorio
reviews.text: I recently moved to Austin and was looking for something affordable for me and my family thank God I found Wildcreek! Great place to start if you are getting your first apartment or low income. They also have referral bonus which helps put some extra cash in my pocket.

reviews.author: Edelmira Barrera
reviews.text: Wonderful place to live. Office staff always so kind to help out in any situation. Since day 1 they have always been so helpful with me and my son! Even though community is not gated i havent had any problems or issue. Staff always have it under control with work orders as well!!!

reviews.author: Owen D
reviews.text: Management knows of severe mild issues. I had no idea how bad mold coukd impact the rest of your life. I showed mold reports I got from 3rd party testing sites and management ignored it. They also lied to me about the water damage and stains on the ceilings when I moved in. I also never got my security deposit back eve

reviews.author: Martha Biss
reviews.text: I have just moved out of Cohabs in NYC and I only have positive things to say! The facilities such as the basement/cinema room, the living room, and the rooftop make it a really sociable and fun place to hang out. I also really liked having a gym downstairs. Living here was definitely a top highlight of my 10 months in

reviews.author: Arya Anand
reviews.text: Living at Cohabs was the best part of NYC for me! I've made some lifelong friendships and am constantly greeted with people who make living in the city feel like a home. Cohabs takes time and consideration to curate people of a similar vibe, and hosts events across the different houses to further curate friendships. Wh

reviews.text: Being in Cohabs has made my transition to living New York so much easier and the amount of friends that I’ve made has been nothing short of remarkable. reviews.author: Terry J
reviews.text: While the location is amazing and the leasing office personnel are great, the laundry situation here and the state of the units is just beyond horrendous. The pool is really great, my daughter loves it! There’s also a nice little fitness

reviews.author: Jasmine Ni

reviews.author: dominic franceschelli

reviews.author: Jose

reviews.author: Makenzi Short

reviews.author: Andre Leblanc
reviews.text: Everything was very easy moving in and the communication was great! The apartment we have was very nice and spacious. The property manager Ms. Lauren is very helpful and nice too and it was a pleasure to rent with her :)

reviews.author: Federico Musto
reviews.text: I’ve really enjoyed living at 6Nineteen. The best part is definitely the people, both the community and the staff are super friendly and welcoming, which made me feel at home right away. The amenities are also great and really make everyday life easier and more enjoyable. Overall, it’s been a very positive experience.

reviews.author: Julius Mundt

reviews.author: Isabella Rodriguez-Miller
reviews.text: The amenities in this apartment are really nice. They do a good job fixing things quickly when needed. I don’t have many issues living here.

reviews.author: Wren Canonica

reviews.author: Ayushmaan Narayan

reviews.text: “Really enjoyed living here. reviews.text: Very beautiful property and helpful staff. Would highly recommend living here

reviews.author: Jaida P
reviews.text: Ricky in maintenance is the best. Can always count on him to get it done or keep you up to date on the process.

reviews.author: Elizabeth Fallon
reviews.text: This review is for the maintenance staff of these apartments. We very much appreciate his work!

reviews.author: Parmida mirhashemi

reviews.author: Aran McDonald

reviews.author: JJJ

reviews.author: Matthew Martin

reviews.author: Megan McLaughlin

reviews.text: Very mixed environment, lived there two years. Maintenance guy Dallas was great the whole time, but stretched thin with multiple properties. It’s a decent location for getting to know Seattle. Streetcar stop close and easy walk to Capitol Hill. It’s right across from Seattle U and by all the hospitals. Downsides are it

reviews.author: Michelle Tran
reviews.text: The leasing and management staff did an impeccable job at getting me the apartment I wanted in such short notice. Not only were they able to provide me with options and tours even with my hectic schedule, they also were very kind and helpful in the process! Wouldn't trust anyone else to rent from!

name: Melrose Apartments
address: 4065 W Pine Blvd #210-218, St. Louis, MO 63108, USA

reviews.text: Fecrete the painter worked very hard on patching my ceilings. Great quality look in a timely fashion. Fecrete has the greatest personality. Thanks for job well done

reviews.text: Very friendly people and excellent mainetence wanna thank angel Vargas and Smitty for getting my power issues resolved over the weekend. Jason the electrician was very professional from jw Kelley electric company to restore the power issues keep up the good work guys.

reviews.text: Incredible amenities! From the massive "Game Zone" (pool, arcade, air-hockey) to the outdoor shuffleboard and badminton, there’s always something to do. The co-working spaces and media room are perfect for remote work. Luxury living at its finest with a 24/7 staff that actually cares.

reviews.author: Phyllis Elliott
reviews.text: I've lived in this building for six months and things have gone well for the most part. The thing that mostly irritates me, is that there is NO water pressure or hot water and if anyone else in the building runs water, it turns extremely cold. I've heard rumors that the water heaters will be replaced, but have heard no

reviews.text: Lived here during Graduate Shool, was great the first year. Afterwards, the place went downhill fast. A lot of illegal activity and safety issues. reviews.author: Lily Brassey
reviews.text: I booked here for a 10-week internship and it worked out great. Quick access to downtown, washer/dryer in-unit, and no stress about setup. Great temporary spot if you’re working in the city.

reviews.author: Jhon
reviews.text: A good place. The biggest value is speed and simplicity.

reviews.author: Arthur Greenberg
reviews.text: Awesome building. Really excited for the pool this summer!

One thing that’s surprising, though, is that the building doesn’t highlight one of its biggest advanta

reviews.author: Broderick Zara
reviews.text: Lived at June Homes for three months and it delivered exactly what I needed. I honestly can't imagine doing two or three years in a coliving setup, but for a short- term arrangement while you find your footing, it's genuinely fine. Nothing spectacular, nothing terrible. reviews.author: Esther Orla
reviews.text: They’re very responsive, easy to work with, and flexible when it comes to individual needs. Whenever I had a question or an issue, it was addressed quickly, usually within a day. I’d definitely recommend them to anyone searching for a new place to live.

reviews.text: Beautiful, brand new property coming to Sarasota! Amazing location!

reviews.text: Just moved in. Great location to downtown Sarasota. This is a new complex with everything being brand new in the apartment. Modern amenities included in the complex. I am glad I moved here.

reviews.author: Lunyiu Nie
reviews.text: The noise in this neighborhood is unbearable — loud, disruptive music blasting past midnight until 2 AM. I’ve made multiple complaints over the past few years, but nothing has ever been resolved. Absolutely do not recommend living here.

reviews.text: the area around was useful for student life

reviews.text: I spent the last year living at 1824 Belmont Rd in the 1 bedroom, 1 bathroom basement unit. The unit was well priced, very spacious (720 SF), and was equipped with updated kitchen and bathroom fixtures. The biggest flaw is this specific unit has very limited closet space. reviews.author: Carly Simpson
reviews.text: The people here are so nice and informative! I had questions on Instagram and they were so helpful and guided me to their waitlist. I’ll definitely be interested in learning more!

reviews.author: Charlene Downs
reviews.text: Charming block. Limited parking. Can be loud at night. Historic building so beware of paper thin walls. No in unit laundry. No garbage disposal. Secure entry.

reviews.text: I really enjoy having my own kitchen and the apartment is very spacious and the furniture is really nice! The only two things I dislike are that it's really close to Greek row and can get loud during the weekends, and this building seems to have issues with their alarm systems. reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: The location was fantastic, surrounded by trendy cafes, shops, and entertainment venues. The neighbourhood is also pleasant and beautiful. Most importantly, City College of New York is within a short walk.

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: The facilities were top-notch, with state-of-the-art amenities and stylishly furnished rooms. Overall, I had an exceptional experience and would highly recommend this accommodation to any student seeking a luxurious and convenient place to live in New York.

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: The property has super speed wifi if students need to stream anything and a TV to enjoy their stay. Every room is spacious and cosy enough to have sound sleep and keep all the stuff. Thanks to team amber for helping me in the process to get here.

reviews.author: Brendon Burke
reviews.text: Overall, the actual apartments are nice. I was happy with that side of things for the most part, but I would be very careful when touring to check on the location of your bedroom. There are some apartments (mine, apt 2E, included) with bedrooms that are directly adjacent to a community deck and a fire escape where you

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: The best place, always clean and quiet, very luxurious and comfortable for students that are new to Chicago.

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: The management here is very nice and supportive. They are understanding of hard circumstances when one goes through them. Most importantly, whenever anything is wrong in the apartment, The maintenance supervisor is very quick to visit the apartment and take care of the problem right away

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: Great experience here, really nice accommodation, rooms are fully furnished and very clean. Really good service and friendly staff. Great location as well, very convenient. Highly recommended!!

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: Great place! The place honestly made me feel like home in no time. The on-site staff was lovely and helped me through all my questions, as well as made sure to provide me with everything I need.

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: My booking agent from Amber recommended me this place and I'm so grateful I took it. He even made sure my entire moving in process went smoothly and answered all my questions with patience.

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: This is an extremely cool property to stay at!! The interiors are designed well and it also has a huge communal lounge where everyone can sit and relax after a long day.

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: pretty student-friendly overall in terms of setup

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: My room is very cozy. The staff is friendly and helpful. The area around the building is totally safe. There is room to study and social spaces to enjoy your free time. Thanks to team amber for recommending me this amazing place and also for helping me with the booking process.

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: Excellent student housing and facilities!! The staff is genuinely nice and has assisted me with any problems I've encountered. The overall atmosphere of the building is pleasant and peaceful, and I feel safe staying here. The surrounding area is also fantastic, with multiple shops within walking distance.

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: Living at Kings Street has been a wonderful experience. Stayed here for almost a year and haven’t faced any issues so far. The laundry facilities are so great. Made a lot of new friends in the games area playing snooker. Enjoying my stay so far and would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for student accommodati

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: This place feels more like a home than student housing. The apartment is modern and well-furnished. Plus, there's a small park nearby, which is great for a morning jog or evening walk. The university is located nearby, and there are plenty of great hangout spots to enjoy.

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: This is my best find in all of the student housing I've seen so far. It’s a bit on the expensive side for my budget, but well worth it. The Cafe Joe Coffee Company is my go-to place for study sessions and doodling. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Verified resident
reviews.text: During summer, the backyard is a paradise. Though the lofts are a tad small, their clever design compensates for it. I feel very comfortable here as the housekeeping and basic amenities are included. Plus this place is situated very close to the main campus buildings.
